# Bookmarks Page Pattern

Model this page on the reference site's bookmarks route: a living inbox plus archive for what the person is reading, watching, or tracking.

Purpose:

- capture what the person is paying attention to right now
- distinguish transient inputs from polished recommendations

Reference structure:

- page title and short explanatory sentence
- small topical filter chips when tags support them
- search input
- inbox/archive tabs
- dense table with title, summary, tags, source, and date
- direct external-link affordance
- optional click-through to an internal note when a note exists

Design cues:

- keep the page light and utility-driven
- make saved items easy to triage
- allow metadata to stay secondary to the title
- this page should feel closer to a thoughtful notebook than a media gallery

Good dynamic behaviors:

- filters
- search
- direct open to external source
- optional open-note or detail-page behavior
- tab count badges

Keep it profile-agnostic:

- do not assume a specific reading workflow
- use only the metadata the user actually has
- preserve the distinction between unread/live items and processed/archive items when that workflow exists
